Special Events
Any of the following events can occur while the characters are staying in Vallaki.
Festival of the Blazing Sun

The Festival of the Blazing Sun takes place three days after the characters first arrive in Vallaki. You can delay the festival if the characters get waylaid or drawn elsewhere, or you can advance the timeline if the characters seem to be in a hurry.

Under threatening skies, a parade of unhappy children dressed as flowers trudges through the muddy streets, leading the way for a group of sorry-looking men and women carrying a ten-foot-diameter wicker ball. The burgomaster and his smiling wife, who holds a sad bouquet of wilting flowers, follow the procession on horseback. As weary spectators watch from their stoops, the ball is borne to the town square. There, it is hoisted and hung from a

fifteen-foot-high wooden scaffold, and townsfolk take turns splashing it with oil. Before the wicker sun can be set ablaze, the sky tears open in a sudden downpour. "All will be well!" cries the burgomaster as he brandishes a sputtering torch and marches defiantly through the rain toward the wicker ball, only to have his torch go out as he thrusts it into the sphere.
A singular laugh erupts from the crowd, drawing the burgomaster's fiery gaze as well as gasps from the townsfolk.

The laugh comes from Lars Kjurls (LG male human guard), a member of the town militia. The other guards are aghast at Lars's ill-timed outburst. The burgomaster immediately has Lars arrested for "spite." Unless the characters intervene, Lars is bound at the ankles and wrists, then dragged behind the burgomaster's horse for the "amusement" of all. The burgomaster rides the horse himself.
Developments

If the characters challenge the burgomaster in any way, he orders them banished from Vallaki. If they protest, he orders the guards to arrest them, deprive them of their weapons, and force them out of Vallaki at sword point.
If the characters lose their weapons, the Keepers of the Feather (see area N2) eventually steal back the weapons and return them to the characters.
If the guards fail in their duty, the burgomaster retreats to his mansion and the townsfolk flee to their homes, giving the characters free rein in town.
Tyger, Tyger

Karl and Nikolai Wachter (see areas N2 and N4) are young, foolish men from a proud noble family. The drunken brothers sneak into Arasek's Stockyard (area N5) while everyone else is attending the festival in the town square. On a dare, one of them rocks the wagon. The saber-toothed tiger locked inside becomes enraged and smashes through the wagon door. The characters and everyone else in town hear the screams of the young men as the tiger escapes. The tiger flees the stockyard without harming the Wachters and begins to prowl the streets, looking for an escape. Reports of a tiger running loose in the streets ruin the festival and send townsfolk scurrying for their homes.

The saber-toothed tiger doesn't harm anyone until it takes damage, whereupon it attacks the perceived source of the damage. If he is still alive, Izek Strazni gathers six town guards and hunts down the beast with the intention of killing it. Meanwhile, Rictavio does his best to lure the beast back to his wagon while assuring townsfolk that it won't harm them.
Development

If he's still in power, the burgomaster conducts an investigation to find out where the tiger came from. Guards and local witnesses are questioned. The Wachter boys feign innocence, insisting that they were at the festival, but Gunther and Yelena Arasek (area N5) admit to hearing "evil growls" and scratching sounds coming from inside the carnival wagon parked in their stockyard. When pressed, the Araseks admit to seeing the wagon's "weird owner" routinely drop food into the wagon through a hatch in the roof. They also confess that the half-elf paid them for their silence.

After the burgomaster learns that the tiger belongs to Rictavio, he commands his guards to arrest the mysterious bard. If Rictavio thinks the characters can help him, he asks them to distract the burgomaster and the guards while he gathers his horse, wagon, and tiger (in that order). If the characters ask Rictavio where he plans to go, he tells them about an old tower to the west where he can lie low (see chapter 11, "Van Richten's Tower").
Lady Wachter's Wish

Ernst Larnak (see area N4) begins shadowing the characters. Characters who have a passive Wisdom (Perception) score of 14 or higher notice him doing so. If they confront him, he claims that he keeps a watchful eye on all strangers, though he doesn't mention the name of his employer. If the characters threaten him, he backs off and reports to Lady Wachter after he believes he's not being watched or pursued.

Lady Wachter is looking for powerful allies to help her oust the burgomaster. If Ernst tells her that he thinks the characters fit the bill, Lady Wachter has Ernst or her sons invite the characters to a private dinner at Wachterhaus. During the dinner, Lady Wachter determines whether the characters have the ability and the resolve to crush the baron.
If the characters refuse her invitation, or if they profess to be enemies of Strahd, Lady Wachter marks them as her enemies and sets out to destroy them without incriminating herself.
Development

Once she determines that the characters are her enemies, Lady Wachter hands Ernst a bag of 100 gp (taken from area N4q) and instructs him to deliver it to the Vistani camp outside town (area N9), along with a letter from her that asks the Vistani to dispose of the characters once they have left town. The Vistani burn the letter after reading it, as per Lady Wachter's request.
If the characters have rescued Arabelle (see chapter 2, area L), the Vistani return Lady Wachter's gold to Ernst and do nothing.

Otherwise, a Vistana bandit watches the road east of Vallaki and reports back to camp if the characters are sighted leaving. The Vistani, worried that the characters might be more than a match for them, send one emissary on horseback to race ahead of the characters and inform Strahd. If Arrigal is alive, he makes the ride himself. Otherwise, the rider is a young Vistana bandit named Alexei (see area N9c).
St. Andral's Feast

The characters can prevent this special event from occurring by returning the bones of St. Andral to the church (area N1) or by destroying the vampire spawn hiding in the coffin maker's shop (area N6). If the characters stay in Vallaki for three days or more and don't retrieve the bones or destroy the vampire spawn, Strahd visits the coffin maker's shop the following evening and orchestrates an attack on the church.

The vampire spawn begin the attack that night. They cling to the outer walls and roof of St. Andral's church while four swarms of bats enter the church through the belfry and terrify the congregation. As the townsfolk flee the church, the vampire spawn leap down and attack them.

During the chaos, Strahd enters the church in bat form, then reverts to vampire form and attacks Father Lucian. Unless the characters intervene, Strahd kills the priest before returning to Castle Ravenloft.

If Father Lucian dies, locals bury his body in the church cemetery, whereupon it rises the following night as a vampire spawn under Strahd's control. If Rictavio (area N2) learns of the priest's death, he suggests that the characters burn the priest's body to ensure that it doesn't rise from the dead.
Development

The attack on St. Andral's church terrorizes and demoralizes the town. After a few days, fear turns to misdirected rage as townsfolk blame the burgomaster. Baron Vallakovich's "All will be well!" mantra can't protect him from their wrath. Barring intervention by the characters, the burgomaster's mansion is set ablaze, and the baron, his wife, and his son are dragged to the town square, thrown in the stocks, and stoned to death. If he is alive, Izek Strazni flees the town to avoid a similar fate. Where he hides is up to you, but likely locations include Old Bonegrinder (chapter 6), Argynvostholt (chapter 7), or the ruins of Berez (chapter 10).

If the characters thwart the attack on the church and protect Father Lucian, Strahd pays a visit to Wachterhaus (area N4) and there composes a letter, which he asks Lady Wachter to deliver to the characters. The letter is written in Strahd's hand and extends an invitation to the characters to come to Castle Ravenloft. Lady Wachter orders her spy, Ernst Larnak, or one of her sons to take the letter to the characters. If the characters open and read it, show the players "Strahd's Invitation" in appendix F. If the characters head toward the castle, they have no threatening random encounters on the way.

Elflume Herb
Elflume Herb is a mystical and rare plant that is highly sought after by alchemists, herbalists, and those seeking a deep sense of relaxation and connection with nature in the fantasy world.

Description: Elflume Herb is a delicate, fern-like plant with iridescent, silver-blue leaves and small, star-shaped blossoms that emit a soft, soothing glow. The leaves and blossoms contain a unique compound that, when dried and prepared, produces a calming and euphoric effect when consumed. It is often harvested by moonlight to enhance its potency.

Elflume Herb is known for its enchanting aroma, which carries a hint of earthy tones, wildflowers, and a touch of ethereal sweetness. When lit or prepared in various forms, the scent lingers in the air, creating a tranquil and otherworldly ambiance.

Consuming Elflume Herb induces a sense of relaxation, heightened sensory perception, and a deep connection to the natural world. It is often used in rituals, meditation, and alchemical brews. The effects can vary depending on the preparation and the individual's sensitivity, but it is generally regarded as a source of tranquility and introspection in the fantasy realm.

Baldur's Bones

Baldur's Bones is a popular dice game in taverns. Each player requires several six-sided dice. The rules are as follows: Each player puts the agreed ante in the pot Each player rolls three dice. Play then proceeds clockwise around the table, with the host of the game going last On their turn, a player can choose to "stand" or "roll." If the player stands, the next player can take a turn. A player who rolls takes an additional die and rolls it. If the total of their dice exceeds 21, they "bust" and are out of the game. Otherwise, they can keep rolling additional dice until they either stand or break After everyone has had a turn, the highest point total (excluding players who busted) wins the game and takes the pot
Important NPCs Strahd von Zarovich

Strahd von Zarovich is an imposing figure, exuding an aura of power and menace that commands attention. He stands tall, his physique lean yet strong, a testament to his martial prowess. His skin is pale, almost translucent, giving him an otherworldly appearance. His face is sharp and angular, with high cheekbones and a pronounced jawline. His eyes are perhaps his most striking feature - deep-set and crimson, they burn with an intense, predatory gaze that seems to pierce one's very soul. His hair is jet black, slicked back from his forehead, contrasting starkly with his pallid complexion. Strahd is typically seen in opulent attire befitting his noble status. He favors dark colors, often seen in a finely tailored black suit adorned with silver accents. Around his neck, he wears a medallion bearing the crest of his family - a stylized dragon. His presence is further accentuated by a flowing, crimson-lined black cape that billows dramatically behind him. Despite his aristocratic appearance, there's an undeniable air of danger about Strahd. His movements are precise and predatory, and his voice is a low, hypnotic drawl that can shift from charming to terrifying in an instant. His mere presence is enough to send a chill down anyone's spine, a chilling reminder of the power he wields.

Pantheon
Genno, the True Form of Creator (True Neutral)

Physical Description: Genno appears as a colossal, ethereal figure, with a body made of intertwining vines, trees, and elements of the natural world. Their face is obscured by a luminous, shifting veil of leaves, symbolizing the ever-changing nature of creation. Genno's arms are like massive tree branches that reach out to touch the world, imbuing it with life. They emanate a serene, calming energy.

Explanation: Genno represents the impartial and balanced essence of creation. They exist as the unifying force behind all life, emphasizing the interconnectedness of all living beings. Genno's presence ensures that the world maintains equilibrium and harmony.
Gento, the Aspect of Civility (Lawful Good)

Physical Description: Gento takes on the form of a regal, golden-skinned humanoid figure with luminous, benevolent eyes. They wear flowing robes adorned with intricate symbols and are often depicted holding a book or scroll. A radiant aura surrounds them, casting a warm and comforting light.

Explanation: Gento is the embodiment of civilization, enlightenment, and all that is virtuous in society. They exist to inspire and guide mortals in their pursuit of knowledge, justice, and diplomacy. Gento's presence encourages the development of culture, language, and the betterment of the world.
Gresso, the Aspect of Deviation (Chaotic Evil)

Physical Description: Gresso is a monstrous and twisted being with a body composed of dark storm clouds and shadowy tendrils. Their eyes gleam with malevolent intent, and their presence is accompanied by thunder and lightning. Gresso's form is ever-changing and grotesque, reflecting the unpredictable nature of their domain.

Explanation: Gresso embodies the chaos and destruction inherent in the world. They exist as a force of upheaval, driving the world towards change, sometimes for better but often for worse. Gresso's influence gives rise to storms, calamities, and the creation of undead, as they represent the unsettling aspects of existence.
Senzos, the True Form of Curiosity (Chaotic Good)
Physical Description: Senzos appears as a fiery and ever-changing entity, resembling a swirling, multicolored tornado of flames. His eyes are bright, playful orbs, and he is often
depicted with a mischievous grin. His form is both ethereal and chaotic, with sparks and embers dancing around him.

Explanation: Senzos embodies the spirit of unbridled curiosity and adventure. He represents the desire to explore, learn, and embrace the unknown. Senzos is the patron of creativity, inventiveness, and the burning passion to uncover new knowledge and experiences.
Raggios, the Aspect of Knowledge (Chaotic Neutral)

Physical Description: Raggios is a figure of boundless energy and erratic motion. He appears as a being composed of constantly shifting, ever-shifting geometric shapes and patterns. His eyes are like swirling vortexes of information, and his form exudes a sense of unpredictability and randomness.

Explanation: Raggios embodies the unpredictable and enigmatic aspects of knowledge. He encourages exploration, experimentation, and thinking outside the box. Raggios represents the pursuit of knowledge without constraints, even if it means embracing chaos and ambiguity.
Celatos, the Aspect of Secrets (Chaotic Evil)

Physical Description: Celatos is a shadowy figure, shrouded in darkness and cloaked in secrecy. His eyes are concealed beneath a hood, and his presence is often accompanied by eerie whispers. He moves silently and stealthily, leaving a sense of unease in his wake.

Explanation: Celatos embodies the darker side of curiosity, focusing on secrets, subterfuge, and deceit. He thrives on hidden knowledge and the manipulation of truth. Celatos represents the shadowy corners of existence where hidden agendas and concealed truths lurk, making him both a source of intrigue and danger in the world.
Malignus, the True Form of Chaos (Chaotic Evil)

Physical Description: Malignus takes on the form of an ever-shifting, monstrous entity, constantly morphing between nightmarish forms. Their body is a swirling vortex of darkness, and their eyes are chaotic voids that seem to devour all light. Malignus's presence is disorienting, and they emit an unsettling, discordant energy.

Explanation: Malignus represents the raw and destructive force of chaos. They are the source of disorder, turmoil, and malevolence in the world. Malignus's influence spawns demons and other chaotic entities, sowing destruction and upheaval wherever they tread.
Licentus, the Aspect of Randomness (Chaotic Neutral)

Physical Description: Licentus appears as an unpredictable and enigmatic figure, resembling a being of constantly shifting probability. They have a playful and mischievous demeanor, with eyes that flicker with uncertainty. Their form seems to waver between existence and non-existence, reflecting the unpredictable nature of chance.

Explanation: Licentus embodies the capriciousness of randomness and unpredictability. They are the patron of chance, whimsy, and the unexpected. Licentus encourages embracing the chaos of life's twists and turns, where fate is as likely to bring fortune as it is to bring calamity.
Fortunus, the Aspect of Luck (Chaotic Good)

Physical Description: Fortunus takes on a charismatic and vibrant form, exuding an aura of warmth and positivity. They have sparkling eyes that radiate joy and goodwill. Fortunus is often depicted with an enchanting smile, bringing a sense of hope and serendipity to those in their presence.

Explanation: Fortunus represents the brighter side of chaos, emphasizing good luck, fortune, and serendipity. They are the embodiment of the belief that even in the midst of chaos, opportunities for love and happiness can be found. Fortunus's influence encourages acts of kindness, love, and spontaneous acts of goodwill.
Halosia, the True Form of Conquest (Lawful Evil)

Physical Description: Halosia appears as a towering, regal figure, draped in dark, imposing armor adorned with symbols of dominance and control. Her eyes burn with an unyielding ambition, and she wields a massive, sinister blade that glows with a malevolent energy. Halosia's presence is authoritative and oppressive, exuding an aura of domination.

Explanation: Halosia is the personification of conquest, power, and control. She thrives on the subjugation of others and seeks to expand her dominion relentlessly. Halosia's influence is felt in the realms of death and among the devils, where her iron rule holds sway.
Proelia, the Aspect of War (Neutral Evil)

Physical Description: Proelia is a fearsome and battle-hardened figure, with scars and battle-worn armor that bear testament to countless conflicts. His eyes are cold and calculating, and his presence exudes an aura of bloodlust and violence. Proelia wields a jagged, brutal weapon, stained with the blood of foes.

Explanation: Proelia embodies the relentless and brutal aspects of war. He revels in bloodshed, conflict, and the pursuit of power through force. Proelia's domain is marked by aggression and warfare, as he seeks to claim victory and dominion over others through martial prowess and cunning tactics.
Imperia, the True Form of Control (Lawful Good)

Physical Description: Imperia takes on a serene and majestic form, appearing as an ethereal figure with a regal countenance. Her skin shimmers with a silvery glow, reminiscent of the moon, and her eyes exude an aura of wisdom and benevolence. Imperia is often depicted holding a staff or scepter, symbolizing her authority.

Explanation: Imperia represents the ideal of control as a force for order and harmony. She governs the balance of power and responsibility, ensuring that order is maintained without oppressive tyranny. Her domain is closely tied to the moon, symbolizing the cycles of time and the influence of celestial bodies on the world.
Lexia, the Aspect of Law (Lawful Neutral)

Physical Description: Lexia embodies the embodiment of order and structure. She appears as a figure of perfect symmetry and precision, clad in robes of intricate designs and patterns. Her eyes radiate a keen, calculating intelligence, and she often carries a tome or scroll containing the laws of the universe.

Explanation: Lexia personifies the strict and impartial aspects of law. She is the guardian of rules, governance, and the balance between chaos and control. Lexia's influence extends to the realms of magic, as she upholds the principles that govern the arcane and mystical forces in the world.
Castia, the Aspect of Punishment (Lawful Evil)

Physical Description: Castia appears as a stern and imposing figure, draped in dark, foreboding robes. Her eyes are sharp and judgmental, and her presence carries an air of strict discipline and authority. She wields a scepter adorned with symbols of punishment and consequence.

Explanation: Castia embodies the darker side of control, focusing on punishment, retribution, and the enforcement of law through fear. She seeks to maintain control through strict adherence to rules, and her dominion is characterized by a system of penalties and consequences for those who transgress.
